fix the nil pointer bug
❯ fix the nil pointer bug
⠋ Thinking...⠙ Thinking...⠹ Thinking...⠸ Thinking...
I'll fix the nil pointer dereference in the agent manager.

📂 Read internal/agent/manager.go

// ... existing code ...

The issue is on line 45 where `agents` can be nil when no agents are connected.

✏️ Edit internal/agent/manager.go

 func (m *Manager) ListAgents() []Agent {
     m.mu.RLock()
     defer m.mu.RUnlock()
-    return m.agents
+    if m.agents == nil {
+        return []Agent{}
+    }
+    return m.agents
 }

The fix ensures `ListAgents()` always returns an empty slice instead of nil. This prevents nil pointer dereference when the caller iterates over the result, and ensures JSON marshaling produces `[]` instead of `null`.
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

❯
